name: Abdul Rahman ibn Masood
title: Loyal Son
shortDescription: The son of Mas'ud ibn Hajjaj who fled from Umar Sa'd's army with his father and was martyred alongside Imam Hussain (AS).
fullStory:
Abdul Rahman ibn Mas'ud ibn Hajjaj al-Taymi was a strong and faithful young man who followed in his father's footsteps on the path of devotion to Wilayah.

He, along with his father, was seemingly dispatched to Karbala with the Kufan army, but their hearts were with Hussain (AS). Abdul Rahman managed to join the camp of light alongside his father and took his place among the Imam's loyal companions.

Martyrdom alongside one's father is one of the most beautiful manifestations of Ashura. On Ashura, with the start of Ibn Ziyad's general attack, Abdul Rahman stood bravely and defended the tents.

Eventually, this devoted youth fell in blood shoulder-to-shoulder with his brave father during the first attack (morning battle), and his soul flew to heaven. The infallible Imam respectfully mentions his name alongside his father in Ziyarat al-Rajabiyya.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلَى عَبْدِ الرَّحْمانِ بْنِ مَسْعُودِ بْنِ حَجّاجٍ...
quote: (Ziyarat al-Rajabiyya:) Peace be upon Abdul Rahman ibn Mas'ud ibn Hajjaj...
